messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: Hi, I figured I should start a thread for my meshcore-bot project, built with meshcore_py. This started as a casual project for our local mesh and has rapidly become popular with people on Pugetmesh/Salishmesh who want an acknowledgement of their test. A couple key commands are test, which responds with an acknowledgement, path, and final SNR/RSSI, and time. ack [user] | 6d,7e,5f | SNR: 1.5 dB | RSSI: -92 dBm | Received: 18:19:27 Path returns the path with repeater names included (from either the local contacts database or a prefix api from sites like map.w0z.is) Other commands include a channels list, prefix lookup dice/roll, hf conditions, ping/pong, solar conditions, bot stats, sun/moon status, weather (currently NOAA dependent), sports, jokes. Commands are built as plugins and can be developed as self-contained files. https://github.com/agessaman/meshcore-bot

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: I've been working on enabling internationalization in the bot. I've extracted most of the command-related strings and I've created some (probably wildly unnatural) translations using computer translations. I've also added an alternative Weather command that uses OpenMeteo and has coverage for the whole world. I've also added command overrides. If you want to make modifications to a command, you can duplicate the file into ./modules/commands/alternatives and they will override existing commands with the same name field. If you change name = "gwx" to name = "wx" the example global weather command will unload the standard wx command in favor of your replacement. I added the option to use the path guessing logic to estimate path lengths in test messages. New placeholders are {path_distance} for the estimated total path traveled, and {firstlast_distance} for the distance between the first and last known repeater. I also fixed an issue with the packet stream not working in the web viewer.
messages.content_html: I've been working on enabling internationalization in the bot. I've extracted most of the command-related strings and I've created some (probably wildly unnatural) translations using computer translations. I've also added an alternative Weather command that uses OpenMeteo and has coverage for the whole world. I've also added command overrides. If you want to make modifications to a command, you can duplicate the file into ./modules/commands/alternatives and they will override existing commands with the same name field. If you change name = "gwx" to name = "wx" the example global weather command will unload the standard wx command in favor of your replacement. I added the option to use the path guessing logic to estimate path lengths in test messages. New placeholders are {path_distance} for the estimated total path traveled, and {firstlast_distance} for the distance between the first and last known repeater. I also fixed an issue with the packet stream not working in the web viewer.

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: Yes, commands have a setting that is part of the base command class. The default behavior is to inherit the global permissions list, but if channels = <list> is set for any command it overrides that list. The alternative behaviors are noted in the config.ini.example. Not Set (Commented or Absent): Command uses global monitor_channels from [Bot] section [Joke_Command] # channels = general,#bot,#jokes # Commented = uses global monitor_channels Empty Value: Command becomes DM-only (disabled for all channels) [Joke_Command] channels = # Empty = DM-only mode Channel List: Command restricted to specific channels [Joke_Command] channels = general,#bot,#jokes # Only works in these channels
messages.content_html: Yes, commands have a setting that is part of the base command class. The default behavior is to inherit the global permissions list, but if channels = <list> is set for any command it overrides that list. The alternative behaviors are noted in the config.ini.example. Not Set (Commented or Absent): Command uses global monitor_channels from [Bot] section [Joke_Command] # channels = general,#bot,#jokes # Commented = uses global monitor_channels Empty Value: Command becomes DM-only (disabled for all channels) [Joke_Command] channels = # Empty = DM-only mode Channel List: Command restricted to specific channels [Joke_Command] channels = general,#bot,#jokes # Only works in these channels

messages.text: Okay, I see an issue. I had cleanup happening in both the bot process and the webviewer process. My guess is we ended up with lock contention that took down the webviewer process. I moved some error logging from DEBUG to ERROR, and removed the cleanup from the bot process.

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: Just released meshcore-bot v0.7. This includes a number of usability and stability improvements, better configuration defaults, as well as @Deep-Thought 's changes to the Nix packaging and @crisprCAS9 's magic8 command and {elapsed} placeholder. It also includes optional service plugins for uploading data to @🌲 Tree 's Analyzer, to @recrof 's map, a Meshcore -> Discord bridge, and an implementation of recrof's weather alerts that was requested. It also (hopefully) resolves the issues that were causing the web viewer to crash periodically for some users. Additionally, I've included a minimal config.ini for users who only want the testing features enabled. Full release notes here: https://github.com/agessaman/meshcore-bot/releases/tag/v0.7
messages.content_html: Just released meshcore-bot v0.7. This includes a number of usability and stability improvements, better configuration defaults, as well as @Deep-Thought's changes to the Nix packaging and @crisprCAS9's magic8 command and {elapsed} placeholder. It also includes optional service plugins for uploading data to @🌲 Tree's Analyzer, to @recrof's map, a Meshcore -> Discord bridge, and an implementation of recrof's weather alerts that was requested. It also (hopefully) resolves the issues that were causing the web viewer to crash periodically for some users. Additionally, I've included a minimal config.ini for users who only want the testing features enabled. Full release notes here: https://github.com/agessaman/meshcore-bot/releases/tag/v0.7
embeds_html:
Release v0.7 · agessaman/meshcore-bot
Release Notes New Features Service Plugin Architecture: Implemented service plugin system for background services that run alongside the bot Packet Capture Service: Captures packets from MeshCore ...

messages.text: Hey! I’ve been digging into the repeater status features of the bot. messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: I'm not opposed, but it would take some time to get just right. A few months ago saw how meshcore-ha caused massive issues on our mesh when it did this—one node produced 50% of the mesh's traffic requesting repeater statuses. My goal with the bot project has not been to introduce features that when configured or managed badly turn out to be mesh-breaking. I'll put it in the Under Consideration section of my roadmap document.

# Greeting message template (default for all channels) # Available fields: {sender} - the user's name/ID # For multi-part greetings, separate messages with pipe (|) # Example (single): "Welcome to the mesh, @[{sender}]!" # Example (multi-part): "Welcome to the mesh, @[{sender}]!|This is a great place to chat.|Use !help for commands." greeting_message = Welcome to YQLMesh, @[{sender}]! | PM me !help for commands. # Include mesh network information in greeting # true: Add mesh statistics to greeting (total contacts, repeaters, etc.) # false: Only send the greeting message include_mesh_info = true # Mesh info format template # Available fields: {total_contacts}, {repeaters}, {companions}, {recent_activity_24h} # Example: "\n\nMesh Info: {total_contacts} contacts, {repeaters} repeaters" # Note: Mesh info is appended to the last greeting message part mesh_info_format = \\n Mesh Info: {total_contacts} contacts, {repeaters} repeaters, {recent_activity_24h} active in last 24h

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: Do you have weather_provider = noaa set or weather_provider = openmeteo ? Do other commands that require internet work? solar or gwx or satpass ? If the bot doesn't have internet, that won't work. It tries to query 8.8.8.8:53 for DNS, then tries a query to http://1.1.1.1 via HTTP. If those both fail, it tries to fetch http://www.google.com -- if those are blocked at a firewall or something, the bot will think it is offline.

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: I have the parser working for the latest.txt file. It parses it into 3 hour buckets, and then condenses it into "Lethbridge: Mostly Cloudy 48.7°F 73%RH 3% PoP | H:76.3°F L:48.7°F | Tomorrow: 60.8°F/48.6°F" (these are pulled from the May values that are in the file right now) Users can also request a daily forecast that focuses on high/low and most common conditions, or an hourly forecast that focuses on temps and precip. I'll go through with emojifying it for readability once I have all the bugs worked out.
messages.content_html: I have the parser working for the latest.txt file. messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: Contacts are stored on the device, because otherwise DMs don't work. If you set auto_manage_contacts = bot the bot will automatically add only companions to your radio, and remove the oldest inactive contacts when it needs more space.

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: I'm unlikely to implement an authentication system at this point. I can add it to the list of feature requests. If you are deploying this in a setting where you are concerned about other users on the lan accessing it, don't bind to 0.0.0.0. It can be locally accessible or put it behind a nginx proxy with authentication enabled. If you want to implement authentication, I'll always consider PRs.
messages.content_html: I'm unlikely to implement an authentication system at this point. I can add it to the list of feature requests. If you are deploying this in a setting where you are concerned about other users on the lan accessing it, don't bind to 0.0.0.0. It can be locally accessible or put it behind a nginx proxy with authentication enabled. If you want to implement authentication, I'll always consider PRs.

messages.author: mosssupply
messages.text: High level why GTFS is annoying to work with: it’s all centered around trips rather than stops and routes. As a result you have to do a ton of lookups for every query to dedupe real and scheduled buses.

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: It might be, but the issue was also appropriately staggering the requests. We had some request storms when a user was requesting data too frequently from about 30 repeaters. The lesson was time of day, request interval, and requests per minute all needed to be algorithmic, not user-defined.
messages.content_html: It might be, but the issue was also appropriately staggering the requests. We had some request storms when a user was requesting data too frequently from about 30 repeaters. The lesson was time of day, request interval, and requests per minute all needed to be algorithmic, not user-defined.

messages.author: ThatGuy
messages.text: I'd think it would depend on the usage scenario. Right now, I'm working on deploying a new mesh in our area with a few other people. There are basically no other MC users in our area at the moment other than our group. We're doing a lot of testing. For regular monitoring of established meshes, yes, a longer period of time with flow controls is better. For deployment testing, something much more frequent would be very useful. If I have to wait for 12 hours for the next update, that doesn't tell me much. I want to see trends, time of day differences, etc. I want to be able to tweak things and see if changes are effective. Etc.

(edited)

messages.author: AEMON_GREMM
messages.text: Is there a way, like a conf.d directory, to add plugins that you can load automatically without it being erased on an upgrade?
messages.content_html: Is there a way, like a conf.d directory, to add plugins that you can load automatically without it being erased on an upgrade?

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: If you add other plugins, the installer should leave them in place. It uses a non-destructive rsync. You can add command overrides to the modules/commands/alternatives directory. If you modify a command, drop it in that directory and it will override the one distributed with the bot.
messages.content_html: If you add other plugins, the installer should leave them in place. It uses a non-destructive rsync. You can add command overrides to the modules/commands/alternatives directory. If you modify a command, drop it in that directory and it will override the one distributed with the bot.
messages.reply_html:
AEMON_GREMM
Is there a way, like a conf.d directory, to add plugins that you can load automatically without it being erased on an upgrade?

messages.author: paul / BostonMesh
messages.text: The first two that popped up a few months ago were configured to listen in #test. The owners are unknown and I’m pretty sure that they have not been updated. They were also in spotty locations. I set one up about a month ago that is full featured - and I am in a “reliable” location on the mesh. A more recent one spun up further out West, which has been great to watch - but we run into cascading “prefix” triggers.

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: I just release v0.8.3. This release adds support for parsing multi-byte paths across the bot. Adds a trace command (and its companion tracer , trace reciprocal) that enables the bot to perform traces. Adds a new RandomLine matcher for file-based command responses. Thanks Ian! Adds data-retention rules for all databases and purges old records periodically. Added Earthquake notification service. Thanks davidkjackson54! Adds a Bot → Telegram bridge that enables messages from one or more channels to be posted to Telegram Adds a profanity and hate symbol filter to prevent bad actors on the mesh from getting your Discord banned. Adds support for local service plugins in a separate directory for easy backups. Thanks for the suggestion and testing davidkjackson54! https://github.com/agessaman/meshcore-bot
messages.content_html:
I just release v0.8.3. This release adds support for parsing multi-byte paths across the bot.
  • Adds a trace command (and its companion tracer, trace reciprocal) that enables the bot to perform traces.
  • Adds a new RandomLine matcher for file-based command responses. Thanks Ian!
  • Adds data-retention rules for all databases and purges old records periodically.
  • Added Earthquake notification service. Thanks davidkjackson54!
  • Adds a Bot → Telegram bridge that enables messages from one or more channels to be posted to Telegram
  • Adds a profanity and hate symbol filter to prevent bad actors on the mesh from getting your Discord banned.
  • Adds support for local service plugins in a separate directory for easy backups. Thanks for the suggestion and testing davidkjackson54!
https://github.com/agessaman/meshcore-bot
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: Lïttlëaton
messages.text: awesome! look forward to playing with it tomorrow thanks for all the work!
messages.content_html: awesome! look forward to playing with it tomorrow thanks for all the work!
messages.reply_html:
[PNW] HerculesMulligan
I just release v0.8.3. This release adds support for parsing multi-byte paths across the bot.
  • Adds a trace command (and its companion tracer, trace reciprocal) that enables the bot to perform traces.
  • Adds a new RandomLine matcher for file-based command responses. Thanks Ian!
  • Adds data-retention rules for all databases and purges old records periodically.
  • Added Earthquake notification service. Thanks davidkjackson54!
  • Adds a Bot → Telegram bridge that enables messages from one or more channels to be posted to Telegram
  • Adds a profanity and hate symbol filter to prevent bad actors on the mesh from getting your Discord banned.
  • Adds support for local service plugins in a separate directory for easy backups. Thanks for the suggestion and testing davidkjackson54!
https://github.com/agessaman/meshcore-bot

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: Oh, that's the issue right there. I didn't track that you were using Console. I'm pretty sure that @treehouse ⚡ hasn't implemented the ability to disable the console from connecting to the frame server. When a new connection comes in on the frame server, it evicts the old connection. Basically, the bot is getting into an eviction war with the pyMC_console until it takes down the frame server.
messages.content_html: Oh, that's the issue right there. I didn't track that you were using Console. I'm pretty sure that @treehouse ⚡ hasn't implemented the ability to disable the console from connecting to the frame server. When a new connection comes in on the frame server, it evicts the old connection. Basically, the bot is getting into an eviction war with the pyMC_console until it takes down the frame server.
messages.reply_html:
ZeroPoke
I do have a Heltec V4 device with companion firmware. And you want me to use the Bluetooth connection for the bot? I did notice that when the bot isn't responding to msgs that pyMC console's new chat feature won't connect.

messages.author: treehouse ⚡
messages.text: console is accessing it like any other companion would. I'm not 100% sure if the frame server can support multiple clients ( @[PNW] HerculesMulligan ?) but if it can, it shouldn't be a conflict. it's using the same entry point as any other client would
messages.content_html: console is accessing it like any other companion would. I'm not 100% sure if the frame server can support multiple clients (@[PNW] HerculesMulligan ?) but if it can, it shouldn't be a conflict. it's using the same entry point as any other client would

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: It only supports one client at a time. The binary frame server drains the persisted message data, and any behavior other than evict-on-connect would either need a client id scheme that doesn’t exist in the firmware or would randomly deliver messages to one or the other connected client.
messages.content_html: It only supports one client at a time. The binary frame server drains the persisted message data, and any behavior other than evict-on-connect would either need a client id scheme that doesn’t exist in the firmware or would randomly deliver messages to one or the other connected client.
messages.reactions_html: 1
messages.reply_html:
treehouse ⚡
console is accessing it like any other companion would. I'm not 100% sure if the frame server can support multiple clients (@[PNW] HerculesMulligan ?) but if it can, it shouldn't be a conflict. it's using the same entry point as any other client would

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: Let's say you get absolutely congested, that's 254 prefixes (00 and FF are reserved). 254 * 3 = 762 characters AA, minus one character for the trailing comma is 761 divided by a typical message size of about 140 characters = 6 messages that are literally just a string of numbers. That's not the end of the world, but it's automated.

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: I like the max hops reply filter. I totally see the issue that is designed to solve—both tropoducting and when meshes merge with bots on the same channel. I'm less convinced by the change to the configs, mostly because I'm imagining getting bombarded with requests that are basically, "Why my bot not respond?" that I have to diagnose. In a similar style of change, I have considered adding a yes_i_really_want_to_run_my_bot_on_public_and_i_recognize_that_it_is_probably_really_bad_for_mesh_health_and_also_annoying = true setting before the bot will respond on Public. I haven't played with OpenClaw, but it does probably make sense to include some basic instructions so agents don't screw up user settings. Send over a PR and I'll look at getting it merged. My initial thought with the regions line might be start commented out and get phased in once Liam's regions solution gets a wider rollout and more people are actively using regions.

messages.author: Chris
messages.text: openclaw/nanoclaw now makes it so you just have bots reading your repo and finding instructions and deploying it, so even if you don't want to do AI dev, or take AI PRs, someone is gonna ask openclaw to setup a meshcore bot, and it's gonna goggle it and run hog wild. Most of the AGENTS.md is so when the user asks "this doesn't respond to people after 8 hops" claw doesn't go "You're right, it appears theres a bug, i've removed it (eg, removed the function)" instead if says "There is a cap on how many hops the bot will reply to by default because airtime on the mesh is a limited resource shared with other people, when you get a message with 8 or more hops, it might not be intended for your bot, would you like to change that to 10 if you know your mesh is that big?" Or tries to turn it into an RSS news reader thinking an unscoped private channel is like a private channel on discord
messages.content_html: openclaw/nanoclaw now makes it so you just have bots reading your repo and finding instructions and deploying it, so even if you don't want to do AI dev, or take AI PRs, someone is gonna ask openclaw to setup a meshcore bot, and it's gonna goggle it and run hog wild. Most of the AGENTS.md is so when the user asks "this doesn't respond to people after 8 hops" claw doesn't go "You're right, it appears theres a bug, i've removed it (eg, removed the function)" instead if says "There is a cap on how many hops the bot will reply to by default because airtime on the mesh is a limited resource shared with other people, when you get a message with 8 or more hops, it might not be intended for your bot, would you like to change that to 10 if you know your mesh is that big?" Or tries to turn it into an RSS news reader thinking an unscoped private channel is like a private channel on discord

messages.author: James
messages.text: If you mean this structure it's :- msg.payload = JSON.parse(msg.payload); if (msg.payload.device == ("231")) { var newtemp = msg.payload.temperature_F - 32 ; newtemp = newtemp * 5 ; newtemp = newtemp / 9; newtemp = parseFloat(newtemp).toFixed(2); var hum = msg.payload.humidity; hum = hum - 0; var value ="Date/Time: " + msg.payload.time + "\nTemp: "+ newtemp + " deg.C " + "Humidity: " +hum +"%"; msg.payload = value; return msg;

messages.text: You may need to add whatever user you are running your bot as to the DIALOUT group in order to access serial ports. sudo usermod -a -G dialout <username> You can always cat < /dev/ttyACM0 to see if something is being emitted by the port.

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: If you can drop the file in a place where your bot can access it (either via curl or as a file stream on the disk), I can help build a weather json parser that will work like the wxSim parser. The file format is flexible, and can be adapted to include the data you have available. You can kind of set the baseline for this, and we'll expand on it. For example, this is the OpenWeatherMap format: { "coord": { "lon": -122.08, "lat": 37.39 }, "weather": [ { "id": 800, "main": "Clear", "description": "clear sky", "icon": "01d" } ], "main": { "temp": 282.55, "feels_like": 281.86, "temp_min": 280.37, "temp_max": 284.26, "pressure": 1023, "humidity": 100 }, "visibility": 10000, "wind": { "speed": 1.5, "deg": 350 }, "clouds": { "all": 1 }, "dt": 1560350645, "name": "Mountain View", "cod": 200 } When the wxjson parser is added and the file is configured we'll set: custom.wxjson.<yourcity> = <file>
messages.content_html: If you can drop the file in a place where your bot can access it (either via curl or as a file stream on the disk), I can help build a weather json parser that will work like the wxSim parser. The file format is flexible, and can be adapted to include the data you have available. You can kind of set the baseline for this, and we'll expand on it. messages.author: James
messages.text: How do I know if I have a one byte repeater? The first byte is 44, but isn't that just the identifier ? I.e. when you do path command? Forgive my ignorance, I'm new at the Job Jay G8NAI
messages.content_html: How do I know if I have a one byte repeater? The first byte is 44, but isn't that just the identifier ? I.e. when you do path command? Forgive my ignorance, I'm new at the Job Jay G8NAI

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: If you have updated a repeater to 1.14.0 or later, it supports multibyte paths. When a user sends a packet with a one-byte path hash, you'll see 44. If they send a message with a two-byte path has, you'll see 44XX, and three-byte hashes will show 44XXXX.
messages.content_html: If you have updated a repeater to 1.14.0 or later, it supports multibyte paths. When a user sends a packet with a one-byte path hash, you'll see 44. If they send a message with a two-byte path has, you'll see 44XX, and three-byte hashes will show 44XXXX.
messages.reactions_html: 1

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: It's in the dev branch now. You'll need to copy two sections from config.ini.example — [MqttWeather] is under the Service Plugins section at the bottom, and there's some new settings under [Weather] toward the top to define your custom response keys (basically tell it what topic to look at for which key).
messages.content_html: It's in the dev branch now. You'll need to copy two sections from config.ini.example — [MqttWeather] is under the Service Plugins section at the bottom, and there's some new settings under [Weather] toward the top to define your custom response keys (basically tell it what topic to look at for which key).

messages.author: [PNW] HerculesMulligan
messages.text: I'm struggling to reproduce this, or find a strong pattern. I checked the 11 bots that are feeding the Cascadia Mesh CoreScope analyzer, and only one is exhibiting this behavior. I suspect that it might be a meshcore_py library issue, because the bot just takes the raw packet value and writes it into the json that goes to the MQTT server. Then CoreScope is parsing that raw value off the radio. Can you help me out with a test # Activate venv first if needed: source .venv/bin/activate pip show meshcore and then maybe: python -c "import importlib.metadata as m; print(m.version('meshcore'))" (edited)
